aids.title: Q-Learning Update
aids.content: **Q = Q + alpha * (R + gamma * MAX - Q)**. Remember: 'Q learns from the MAX future' - that's why it's off-policy!

aids.title: GPS Analogy
aids.content: Q-learning is like a GPS that assumes you'll always take the fastest route from your next location (max), even if you sometimes take detours (epsilon-greedy) to discover new roads.

aids.title: Off-Policy Key
aids.content: The 'max' in Q-learning is what makes it off-policy. It updates toward the BEST possible future, not what you actually did. SARSA uses actual next action = on-policy.

aids.title: Convergence Conditions
aids.content: **V**isit all states-actions. **A**lpha decreases properly. **S**tationary MDP. **T**D targets bounded. 'VAST' conditions for convergence.
